messages.content:
翻译：There are two lines of prior work aimed at minimizing FCT, known as clairvoyant and non-clairvoyant
scheduling. The clairvoyant scheduling [7, 34, 30, 18]
assumes prior knowledge of precise flow size information and uses it to approximate the Shortest Remaining Processing Time (SRPT). This approach can theoretically achieve optimal performance, but is very challenging to be deployed in current DCNs, e.g., requiring too many priority queues [7] or re-factor the entire
TCP/IP stack [34, 30, 18]. The non-clairvoyant scheduling [9, 15] requires no prior flow information and dynamically estimates flow size (e.g., based on the bytes
the flow has sent [9]). While this approach is easy to
implement in practice, it cannot precisely distinguish between large and small flows at the beginning, thus failing
to minimize FCTs for latency sensitive short flows.

目前有两种旨在最小化FCT的先前工作线路，即预知调度和非预知调度。预知调度（[7，34，30，18]）假定对精确的流量大小信息有先前的了解，并利用该信息来近似最短剩余处理时间（SRPT）。这种方法在理论上可以达到最佳性能，但在当前的数据中心网络中很难部署，例如需要太多的优先级队列[7]或重构整个TCP/IP堆栈[34，30，18]。非预知调度（[9，15]）不需要任何先前的流量信息，并动态估计流量大小（例如，基于流量已发送的字节[9]）。虽然这种方法在实践中易于实现，但在开始阶段无法精确区分大型和小型流量，从而无法最小化延迟敏感的短流的FCT。

翻译：To minimize FCT and be practical, we explore a new
design space that lies between existing clairvoyant and
non-clairvoyant scheduling solutions. Rather than relying on precise flow information or no prior flow information at all, we ask whether it is possible to use imprecise
flow information to minimize FCT?
为了最小化FCT并实现实用性，我们探索了一个新的设计空间，介于现有的预知调度和非预知调度解决方案之间。我们不依赖于精确的流量信息或完全没有先前的流量信息，而是问是否可以使用不精确的流量信息来最小化FCT?

翻译：Answering this question requires identifying useful,
yet imprecise, flow information and incorporating it into
flow scheduling. Some existing work [17, 35] employ
machine learning (ML) techniques to estimate per-flow
size but fail to get high accuracy (§2.2). Utilizing imprecise flow information is also very challenging. Simply feeding imprecise information to existing clairvoyant
schedulers significantly degrades their performance [17].
回答这个问题需要识别出有用但不精确的流量信息，并将其纳入流量调度中。一些现有的研究工作[17，35]使用机器学习（ML）技术来估计每个流量的大小，但未能达到高准确度（§2.2）。利用不精确的流量信息也是非常具有挑战性的。简单地将不精确的信息提供给现有的预知调度器会显著降低它们的性能[17]。

翻译：We address these challenges with QCLIMB, a practical flow scheduling solution that uses imprecise flow
information to minimize the FCT in DCNs. QCLIMB
is based on a key observation that although determining
per-flow size of DCN applications can be difficult, it is
possible to accurately estimate each flow’s lower and upper bounds. Our experiments (§2.3) on realistic DCN
workloads have shown that the actual sizes of a majority of flows (> 99.9%) fall within their lower and up bounds estimated by random forest (RF) model. Moreover, we found the actual sizes of small flows are generally close to their lower bounds, while medium and large
flows may have a larger gaps between their actual sizes
and lower bounds. These findings provide an opportunity
to precisely differentiate small flows from large flows
based on their lower bounds, making it possible to prioritize small flows over large ones from the start, rather
than in later stages of transmission.

我们通过QCLIMB解决这些挑战，它是一种实用的流量调度解决方案，利用不精确的流量信息来最小化数据中心网络（DCNs）中的FCT。QCLIMB的基本观察是，虽然确定DCN应用程序的每个流量的大小可能很困难，但可以准确地估计每个流量的下限和上限。我们对实际的DCN工作负载进行的实验表明，大多数流量（> 99.9%）的实际大小都落在由随机森林（RF）模型估计的下限和上限之间（§2.3）。此外，我们发现小的流量的实际大小通常接近其下限，而中等和大型的流量可能存在实际大小与下限之间的较大差距。这些发现为根据下限精确区分小流量和大流量提供了机会，使得从开始阶段就能优先考虑小流量，而不是在传输的后期阶段。

翻译：Based on these findings, we develop a novel scheduling algorithm consisting of two main phases: queueclimbing-up and queue-climbing-down. Each flow is
initially mapped to a priority queue based on its lower
bound. During the queue-climbing-up phase, the flow is
gradually promoted to higher-priority queues based on
its remaining data size relative to the lower bound. If
the flow is not yet completed after the first phase, it enters the queue-climbing-down phase. During this phase,
QCLIMB gradually demotes the flow to lower-priority
queues based on its bytes sent, and once its upper bound
finishes, it is pulled directly to the lowest priority queue.

根据这些发现，我们开发了一种创新的调度算法，由两个主要阶段组成：队列逐渐升级和队列逐渐降级。每个流量最初根据其下限被映射到一个优先级队列中。在队列逐渐升级阶段，根据流量相对于下限的剩余数据大小，该流量逐渐提升到更高优先级的队列中。如果该流量在第一阶段后尚未完成，它将进入队列逐渐降级阶段。在这个阶段，QCLIMB根据流量已发送的字节数逐渐降低其优先级队列，并一旦其上限完成，该流量将直接被拉入最低优先级队列中。

翻译：QCLIMB’s algorithm is effective at prioritizing small
flows over large ones for two reasons: i) small flows are
close to their lower bounds and thus can finish in the first
few higher priority queues during the queue-climbingup phase; and ii) medium and large flows will be transmitted behind small flows during the queue-climbing-up
phase because their lower bounds are relatively larger.
They will also be penalized to the last few lower priority
queues during the queue-climbing-down phase.
QCLIMB算法之所以能够优先考虑小流量而不是大流量，有两个原因：i）小流量接近其下限，因此可以在队列逐渐升级阶段的前几个较高优先级队列中完成；ii）中等和大型流量在队列逐渐升级阶段将在小流量后面传输，因为它们的下限相对较大。它们在队列逐渐降级阶段还将被降至最后几个较低优先级队列中。

翻译：In addition to designing the scheduling algorithm, we
must also tackle practical out-of-order (OOO) issues.
This is because during the queue-climbing-up phase, the
later packets of a flow can enter higher-priority queues
than the earlier ones. The default TCP OOO handling
mechanism considers this event as a packet loss and triggers unnecessary retransmissions. This results in serious performance degradation, especially for small flows
除了设计调度算法之外，我们还必须解决实际的乱序（OOO）问题。这是因为在队列逐渐升级阶段，流量的后面的数据包可能会进入比前面的数据包更高优先级的队列。默认的TCP乱序处理机制将此事件视为数据包丢失，并触发不必要的重传。这导致性能严重下降，特别是对于小流量来说更为明显。

翻译：Addressing the OOO issue needs to overcome two
practical challenges: First, how to differentiate reorderings caused by QCLIMB’s scheduling algorithm from
actual packet loss? Second, for packets reordered by
QCLIMB, how to efficiently reorder them at the receiver
side? Simply relying on the default TCP reordering
mechanism will cause redundant retransmissions.
解决OOO问题需要克服两个实际挑战：首先，如何区分由QCLIMB调度算法引起的重排与实际数据包丢失？其次，对于被QCLIMB重排的数据包，如何在接收端高效地重新排序它们？仅仅依赖默认的TCP重排序机制将导致冗余的重传。

翻译：To tackle the first challenge, we present a prioritybased loss detection mechanism at the receiver (§3.2.1).
The idea is to leverage the fact that the packets carrying
the same priority belonging to the same flow should be in
order, and a gap within the same priority is identified as
a loss. For the second challenge, we take a non-intrusive
approach by customizing ACKs for OOO packets at the
receiver to bypass the default TCP ACKing mechanism.
Through this way, normal TCP ACKs will not be sent for
OOO packets, and the senders have no chance to trigger
redundant retransmissions (§3.2.2). Moreover, with the
customized ACK, the sender can quickly retransmit the
lost packet without waiting for timeout

为了解决第一个挑战，我们在接收端提出了一种基于优先级的丢失检测机制（§3.2.1）。其思想是利用相同优先级的数据包属于同一流的特点，应该是有序的；而在同一优先级中的间隙被认为是丢失。对于第二个挑战，我们采用了非侵入式方法，通过在接收端为OOO数据包定制ACK，绕过默认的TCP ACK机制（§3.2.2）。通过这种方式，对于OOO数据包，不会发送正常的TCP ACK，并且发送端没有机会触发冗余的重传。此外，借助定制的ACK，发送端可以在不等待超时的情况下快速重传丢失的数据包。

翻译：We have implemented a QCLIMB prototype (§4),
which only requires end host implementation and the
built-in function (e.g., strict priority queuing) in existing commodity switches. We implement the scheduling logic and RF model inferencing as Linux kernel
modules, which reside between the NIC driver and the
TCP/IP stack as a shim layer. Further, OOO handling is
implemented within the TCP and IP layers and requires
minimal modifications to the kernel source code but does
not touch the core TCP congestion control code.
我们已经实现了一个QCLIMB原型（§4），它只需要端主机的实现和现有商品交换机中的内置功能（例如，严格的优先级排队）。我们使用Linux内核模块实现了调度逻辑和RF模型推断，这些内核模块位于NIC驱动程序和TCP/IP堆栈之间作为一个垫片层。此外，OOO处理是在TCP和IP层中实现的，并且对内核源代码进行了最小的修改，但不涉及核心TCP拥塞控制代码。

翻译：We build a small-scale 25G testbed with eight servers
and a Barefoot Tofino switch1
, together with large-scale
simulations at 40/100G network, to evaluate the performance of QCLIMB (§5). We f
我们建立了一个小规模的25G测试平台，包括八台服务器和一台Barefoot Tofino交换机，以及在40/100G网络上进行的大规模仿真，以评估QCLIMB的性能（§5）。我们f

翻译：Indeed, researchers have shown the possibility to learn
flow size information from past traces using prevailing
ML techniques [17, 35, 28, 24]. Nevertheless, the estimated flow sizes of ML models are often imprecise. To
validate this point, we use a widespread ML technique—
RF, over three workloads2
: K-Means, PageRank, and
SGD, provided by [17]. Fig. 1 plots the gap between actual and estimated flow sizes for different-scale RF models we trained (i.e., different maximum tree depth d). In
general, a larger d leads to higher prediction accuracy.
However, the gap between actual and estimated flow
sizes always exists. Under d = 10, the mean/maximum
gap can reach 856KB/296811KB, 820KB/333271KB,
and 55KB/54446KB, for the K-Means, PageRank and
SGD workloads, respectively. In particular, for PageRank under d = 10 RF model, 34% of flows have a gap of
over 100KB to their estimated sizes

的确，研究人员已经展示了使用主流的机器学习技术从过去的追踪数据中学习流量大小信息的可能性[17，35，28，24]。然而，机器学习模型估计的流量大小往往不精确。为了验证这一点，我们使用了一种广泛应用的机器学习技术，即随机森林（RF），在三个工作负载上进行了实验：K-Means，PageRank和SGD，这些负载是由[17]提供的。图1绘制了我们训练的不同规模RF模型（即不同的最大树深度d）的实际和估计流量大小之间的差距。一般来说，较大的d会导致更高的预测准确性。然而，实际和估计的流量大小之间的差距始终存在。在d = 10的情况下，对于K-Means，PageRank和SGD工作负载，均值/最大差距分别可以达到856KB/296811KB，820KB/333271KB和55KB/54446KB。特别是对于PageRank在d = 10的RF模型，有34％的流量与其估计大小相差超过100KB。
